objects.name: Analytic 1762
objects.description: An application generates, imports, or accesses asymmetric keypairs (e.g., RSA/ECC), uses a public key to encrypt outbound data or establish encrypted sessions, and transmits resulting ciphertext in structured communication patterns. Detection correlates keypair lifecycle activity + asymmetric crypto API usage + data transformation + background execution context + network transmission, especially when inconsistent with expected application functionality.

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: App invokes asymmetric cryptographic operations (e.g., RSA/ECC keypair generation OR public key encryption OR signature operations) on outbound data buffers

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Keypair generation, import, or access events (public/private key usage) occurring prior to network communication

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: App writes asymmetric-encrypted blobs or encoded ciphertext to local buffers or files prior to transmission

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Asymmetric crypto operations occur while app_state=background OR device_locked=true OR no recent user interaction

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: android:MDMLog
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: App not in approved cryptographic or secure communication category performing keypair + encryption + transmission behavior

x_mitre_mutable_elements.description: Correlation window between keypair usage and outbound communication
Apps expected to use asymmetric cryptography (e.g., secure messaging, VPN, enterprise auth apps)
Whether key generation/encryption should occur only during user interaction
Frequency of keypair generation/import events considered anomalous
Expected variability in payload sizes due to asymmetric encryption overhead
